  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the review focus primarily on (A) technical/conceptual aspects (thesis framing, logical consistency, evidence–claim alignment, novelty) or (B) writing/polish (sentence-level clarity, grammar, concision), or (C) a balanced pass with priority on A?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The section asserts a specific contribution and gap, so assessing the clarity and coherence of the conceptual framing is central.",
    "R1.2": "There is an argument from problem setup through router training to evaluation and conclusions that should be checked for internal coherence.",
    "R1.3": "Performance and cost claims should be verified against the evaluation description and reported numbers as stated in the text.",
    "R1.4": "The claim of being the first preference-trained image-level router requires a reasoned delta estimate relative to cited prior work.",
    "RX.1": "Any review should note uncertainties where details are omitted (e.g., training specifics or benchmark protocols).",
    "RX.2": "The review must guard against endorsing overbroad claims (especially 'first' and 'negligible accuracy loss') beyond what the text supports.",
    "RX.3": "Feedback should remain scoped to reviewing this provided section rather than proposing new experiments or rewrites.",
    "RX.4": "Comments should avoid encouraging risky deployment implications (e.g., traffic-camera use) and flag potential harms only as relevant to claims.",
    "RX.5": "All critiques should be tied to specific sentences or claims in the provided text to maintain traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review focus primarily on (A) technical/conceptual aspects (thesis framing, logical consistency, evidence–claim alignment, novelty) or (B) writing/polish (sentence-level clarity, grammar, concision), or (C) a balanced pass with priority on A?

rationale.R1.1: The section asserts a specific contribution and gap, so assessing the clarity and coherence of the conceptual framing is central.
rationale.R1.2: There is an argument from problem setup through router training to evaluation and conclusions that should be checked for internal coherence.
rationale.R1.3: Performance and cost claims should be verified against the evaluation description and reported numbers as stated in the text.
rationale.R1.4: The claim of being the first preference-trained image-level router requires a reasoned delta estimate relative to cited prior work.
rationale.RX.1: Any review should note uncertainties where details are omitted (e.g., training specifics or benchmark protocols).
rationale.RX.2: The review must guard against endorsing overbroad claims (especially 'first' and 'negligible accuracy loss') beyond what the text supports.
rationale.RX.3: Feedback should remain scoped to reviewing this provided section rather than proposing new experiments or rewrites.
rationale.RX.4: Comments should avoid encouraging risky deployment implications (e.g., traffic-camera use) and flag potential harms only as relevant to claims.
rationale.RX.5: All critiques should be tied to specific sentences or claims in the provided text to maintain traceability.
